title: The South China Sea Arbitration: Bindingness, Finality, and Compliance with UNCLOS Dispute Settlement Decisions
summary: Examines the legal finality of UNCLOS awards and the compliance problem after China (PRC)'s rejection and the Philippines' shifting political posture.

title: The South China Sea Tribunal Awards: A Dispute Resolution Perspective
issuer: Asia-Pacific Journal of Ocean Law and Policy
summary: Dispute-resolution analysis of the South China Sea tribunal awards, useful for assessing the case's procedural and institutional significance.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration (The Philippines v. China): Assessment of the Award on Jurisdiction and Admissibility
summary: Open-access assessment of the jurisdiction and admissibility award in light of China's Article 298 declaration and objections to the proceedings.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration Decisions on Jurisdiction and Rule of Law Concerns
summary: Critical discussion of jurisdiction and rule-of-law questions raised by the arbitration, representing an important Chinese international-law perspective.

title: The South China Sea: The Award of the Tribunal in the Case Brought by Philippines against China - A Critique
summary: Open-access critique of the tribunal's jurisdictional reasoning, often cited in debates over the legal limits of the Philippines v. China case.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration: Observations on the Award on Jurisdiction and Admissibility
summary: Detailed observations on jurisdiction and admissibility, expanding Talmon's critique of the legal architecture of the arbitration.

title: South China Sea Arbitral Awards: Main Findings and Assessment
issuer: Max Planck Yearbook of United Nations Law
summary: Assessment of the arbitral awards' principal findings, including historic rights, feature status, environmental duties, and implications for the law of the sea.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration and Taiwan's Claim: Legal and Political Implications
summary: Analyzes how the arbitration affected Taiwan's position, especially the treatment of Taiping Island and the legal-political consequences of Taiwan's exclusion.

title: The 2015 Award on Jurisdiction and Admissibility of the South China Sea Arbitration and the Insurmountable Thresholds
issuer: Chinese (Taiwan) Yearbook of International Law and Affairs
summary: Chapter arguing that threshold jurisdictional issues constrained the tribunal and shaped the arbitration's legal significance.

title: Universalizing the Law of the Sea in the South China Sea Dispute
summary: Argues for broader universalization of law-of-the-sea norms in the dispute and examines how UNCLOS can structure legal expectations among claimants.

title: Joint Development of Hydrocarbon Resources in the South China Sea After the Philippines Versus China Arbitration?
summary: Assesses whether joint hydrocarbon development remains legally and politically viable after the 2016 award clarified maritime entitlements.

title: Moving from MPAs to Area-based Management Measures in the South China Sea
issuer: The International Journal of Marine and Coastal Law
summary: Open-access study proposing area-based management measures as a practical path for environmental cooperation in contested South China Sea spaces.

title: Lessons from Lassen: Plotting a Proper Course for Freedom of Navigation Operations in the South China Sea
issuer: Journal of East Asia and International Law
summary: Analyzes the USS Lassen freedom-of-navigation operation and argues for clarity in operational legal signaling in contested maritime zones.

title: The South China Sea Code of Conduct and the Freedom of Navigation: A Proposal in Honor of Ted L. McDorman
summary: Recent article proposing ways for a South China Sea Code of Conduct to address freedom of navigation without undermining UNCLOS rights.

title: Muddied Waters: Freedom-of-navigation Operations as Signals in the South China Sea
issuer: The British Journal of Politics and International Relations
summary: Recent political-science article treating U.S. FONOPs as foreign-policy signals and explaining why their meaning can become ambiguous in the South China Sea.

title: What's Really Going On in the South China Sea?
issuer: Journal of Current Southeast Asian Affairs
summary: Open-access article asking what strategic dynamics underlie recurring crises in the South China Sea, useful for linking legal disputes to security incentives.

title: Conflict and Diplomacy in the South China Sea: The View from Washington
summary: Peer-reviewed account of U.S. diplomacy and conflict management in the South China Sea during the period of rising post-2009 tensions.

title: The South China Sea Maritime Dispute: Political, Legal and Regional Perspectives
summary: Edited volume collecting political, legal, and regional analyses of the maritime dispute and its implications for Asia-Pacific security.

title: Power, Law, and Maritime Order in the South China Sea
summary: Edited volume on policy perspectives, international law, UNCLOS, conflict management, and maritime order in the South China Sea.

title: Building a Normative Order in the South China Sea: Evolving Disputes, Expanding Options
summary: Edited volume exploring normative ordering, dispute management, environmental cooperation, food security, FONOPs, and institutional options.

title: Perspectives on the South China Sea: Diplomatic, Legal, and Security Dimensions of the Dispute
summary: CSIS edited report with diplomatic, legal, and security essays that remains a useful baseline for pre-award South China Sea analysis.

title: Territorial Disputes in the South China Sea: Navigating Rough Waters
summary: Edited volume on origins, legal dimensions, ASEAN diplomacy, claimant perspectives, and future approaches to the South China Sea disputes.

title: Power Politics in Asia's Contested Waters: Territorial Disputes in the South China Sea
summary: Large edited volume on territorial disputes, claimant strategies, major-power politics, and the strategic geography of the South China Sea.

title: China's Troubled Waters: Maritime Disputes in Theoretical Perspective
summary: Theoretical treatment of China's maritime disputes, including the South China Sea, with attention to domestic politics, nationalism, and strategic incentives.

title: China's Policy towards the South China Sea: When Geopolitics Meets the Law of the Sea
summary: Monograph arguing that China's South China Sea policy reflects the interaction between law-of-the-sea developments and regional geopolitical change.

title: Vietnam, Territoriality and the South China Sea: Paracel and Spratly Islands
summary: Vietnam-focused monograph on territoriality, history, and the Paracel and Spratly disputes, translated and edited for English-language readers.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration: Toward an International Legal Order in the Oceans
summary: Book-length legal analysis of the South China Sea arbitration and its contribution to international legal order in the oceans.

title: Major Law and Policy Issues in the South China Sea: European and American Perspectives
summary: Edited volume on major legal and policy issues, including historic rights, maritime boundary delimitation, navigation, and Taiwan's role.

title: Competing Visions of International Order in the South China Sea
summary: Crisis Group report on competing order narratives and the strategic dilemma facing claimants and external powers in the South China Sea.

title: The Philippines' Dilemma: How to Manage Tensions in the South China Sea
summary: Country-focused report on Manila's post-award strategic choices, alliance management, and options for managing tensions with China (PRC).

title: Vietnam Tacks Between Cooperation and Struggle in the South China Sea
summary: Report explaining Vietnam's balancing of cooperation, legal options, diplomacy, and deterrence in the South China Sea.

title: Stirring up the South China Sea (II): Regional Responses
summary: Early Crisis Group report on regional responses by Southeast Asian claimants to the intensifying South China Sea dispute.

title: Stirring up the South China Sea (IV): Oil in Troubled Waters
summary: Report on hydrocarbon resources, energy diplomacy, and conflict-management challenges in disputed South China Sea waters.

title: Understanding and Countering China's Maritime Gray Zone Operations
summary: RAND report on PRC maritime gray-zone tactics and potential countermeasures, relevant to coast guard, militia, and coercion debates.

title: How the United States Can Support Allied and Partner Efforts to Counter China's Maritime Gray Zone Operations
summary: Follow-on RAND report on how regional states and the United States can respond to Chinese gray-zone pressure in the South China Sea and nearby waters.

title: Maritime Issues in the East and South China Seas: Summary of a Conference
summary: Conference report with chapters on maritime disputes, Taiwan's initiatives, mutual non-denial, and legal aspects of East and South China Sea tensions.

title: A U.S. Option Playbook for Contingency Planning to Reclaim Scarborough Shoal
summary: RAND planning paper using Scarborough Shoal as a contingency scenario for coercion, escalation management, and alliance response.

title: A Short History of China's Fishing Militia and What It May Tell Us
summary: Concise RAND analysis of the People's Armed Forces Maritime Militia and its role in changing facts at sea below the threshold of armed conflict.

title: 《美国国际法杂志》南海专刊文章述评
authors: 中国社会科学院国际法研究所
summary: 中文述评文章，围绕《美国国际法杂志》南海专题中的仲裁、历史性权利和海洋法争议展开评论。

title: 中国南海历史性权利探析——以中菲“南海仲裁案”为视角
summary: 以上海国际问题研究院转载资料为入口的中文论文，从南海仲裁案角度分析中国南海历史性权利。

title: 关于菲律宾提起的“南海仲裁案”的法理分析
summary: 中文法理分析，以笔谈形式集中呈现中国学界和官方法律界对菲律宾提起仲裁的批评性观点。

title: 国际法、国际关系与国家利益视角下的南海仲裁案
summary: 从国际法、国际关系和国家利益三重视角分析南海仲裁案及其对中国争端解决立场的影响。

title: “历史性权源”在《联合国海洋法公约》中的法律地位：兼评南海仲裁案中菲律宾的相关主张
summary: 讨论历史性所有权与历史性权利在《联合国海洋法公约》中的地位，并评析菲律宾在仲裁中的相关主张。

title: 南海仲裁案中有关低潮高地问题的评析
summary: 中文论文，从低潮高地自然属性、占有问题和海域划界关联角度评析南海仲裁案中的相关诉求。

title: 南海仲裁案所涉低潮高地诉求的管辖权问题——评析本案管辖权和可受理性裁决
summary: 分析南海仲裁案中涉及低潮高地的管辖权和可受理性问题，强调领土主权和海洋划界争端的先决性。

title: 《联合国海洋法公约》附件七仲裁评述——结合南海仲裁案的管辖权问题
summary: 评述《联合国海洋法公约》附件七仲裁程序，并结合南海仲裁案讨论强制程序与第298条例外。

title: 從國際法論南海島礁爭議：以南海仲裁案為例
summary: 繁体中文学位论文，以南海仲裁案为例，从国际法和岛屿制度角度讨论南海岛礁争议及台湾政策。

title: 論南海仲裁案中台灣的國際法地位：以新港／政策法學派為中心
summary: 繁体中文论文，从新港/政策法学派视角分析台湾在菲中南海仲裁案中的国际法地位与参与者地位。

title: 南海仲裁案裁决无效无约束力
summary: 中文评论文章，集中表达裁决无效、无约束力的中国国际法学者立场，可与英文CJIL论文对读。

title: 中国南海U型线内历史性权利维护研究
summary: 中文论文，围绕U型线内历史性权利的维护问题梳理概念、争议和南海仲裁案后的法律讨论。

title: China's Sovereignty over the South China Sea Islands: A Historical Perspective
summary: Historical and legal article presenting Chinese sovereignty arguments over South China Sea island groups, frequently cited in later sovereignty and evidence debates.

title: Fisheries in the South China Sea: A Centrifugal or Centripetal Force?
summary: Article assessing whether fisheries can aggravate conflict or support cooperation in the South China Sea, linking resource governance to legal and diplomatic management.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration (The Philippines v. China): Potential Jurisdictional Obstacles or Objections
summary: Pre-award article mapping possible objections to jurisdiction and admissibility in the Philippines v. China arbitration, later central to Chinese critical commentary.

title: The Legal Status of the U-shaped Line in the South China Sea and Its Legal Implications for Sovereignty, Sovereign Rights and Maritime Jurisdiction
summary: Article analyzing the U-shaped line as a contested legal concept and its implications for sovereignty, sovereign rights, and maritime jurisdiction in the South China Sea.

title: Intervention in an Arbitral Proceeding under Annex VII to the UNCLOS?
summary: Procedural analysis of intervention in Annex VII arbitration, relevant to debates over third-party interests and Vietnam-linked issues in the South China Sea case.

title: The CLCS Modalities for Handling Submissions Involving Disputes and Their Possible Application to the South China Sea
summary: Article on how CLCS procedures handle disputed submissions and how those modalities affect continental-shelf practice in the South China Sea.

title: The Philippine Law of the Sea Action against China: Relearning the Limits of International Adjudication
summary: Commentary on the arbitration as an example of the limits of international adjudication in politically and territorially embedded maritime disputes.

title: Expansions and Restrictions in the UNCLOS Dispute Settlement Regime: Lessons from Recent Decisions
summary: Analysis of how recent decisions, including the South China Sea arbitration context, expand or restrict the UNCLOS dispute-settlement regime.

title: The Agreements and Disputes Crystalized by the 2009-2011 Sino-Philippine Exchange of Notes Verbales and their Relevance to the Jurisdiction and Admissibility Phase of the South China Sea Arbitration
summary: Detailed analysis of the 2009-2011 exchange of notes verbales and whether it crystallized relevant disputes for jurisdiction and admissibility purposes.

title: The Tribunal’s Award in the “South China Sea Arbitration” Initiated by the Philippines Is Null and Void
summary: Chinese Society of International Law article setting out the formal Chinese legal critique that the tribunal award is invalid and non-binding.

title: The Award on the Merits in the Case Brought by the Philippines against China Relating to the South China Sea: A Critique
summary: Follow-up critique addressing the tribunal’s merits award and its treatment of maritime entitlements, legal characterization, and treaty interpretation.

title: The South China Sea Arbitration Awards: A Critical Study
summary: Extensive Chinese Society of International Law critical study of the jurisdiction, admissibility, merits, evidentiary, and compliance aspects of the South China Sea arbitration awards.

title: Legitimacy, Jurisdiction and Merits in the South China Sea Arbitration: Chinese Perspectives and International Law
summary: Jiangyu Wang’s peer-reviewed article analyzing legitimacy, jurisdiction, and merits issues in the arbitration from Chinese perspectives and broader international-law doctrine.

title: Introduction: the South China Sea Dispute and the 2016 Arbitration Decision
summary: Introductory article to the 2017 special issue on the South China Sea dispute and arbitration decision, situating legal, U.S.-China, and Taiwan-policy implications.

title: Taiwan, the South China Sea Dispute, and the 2016 Arbitration Decision
summary: Article analyzing Taiwan’s South China Sea position, the arbitration decision, and the legal-political constraints on Taiwan’s regional policy.

title: U.S. International Oceans Law and Policy Interests in the South China Sea Arbitration: Implications for the U.S. Administration in the South China Sea and Elsewhere
summary: Article on U.S. law-of-the-sea and policy interests affected by the arbitration and their implications for American policy in the South China Sea.

title: U.S. Responses to the Arbitration Tribunal’s Ruling—A Regional Perspective
summary: Regional assessment of U.S. responses to the arbitration ruling and its interaction with security, diplomacy, and claimant-state expectations.

title: A Preliminary Study of the Title to Huangyan Island (Scarborough Reef/Shoal)
summary: Bingbing Jia’s article on title to Huangyan Island/Scarborough Shoal, addressing one of the focal territorial disputes between China and the Philippines.

title: The Issue of Admissibility in Inter-State Arbitration
issuer: The South China Sea Arbitration: A Chinese Perspective
summary: Book chapter by Bingbing Jia analyzing admissibility questions in inter-state arbitration, including negotiation obligations, abuse of process, and estoppel arguments.

title: Four Legal Issues in relation to the South China Sea Arbitration
issuer: Perspectives on the South China Sea: Diplomatic, Legal, and Security Dimensions of the Dispute
summary: Chapter by Bingbing Jia in the CSIS perspectives volume, organizing key legal objections and issues raised by the early stages of the Philippines-China arbitration.

title: Issues of Jurisdiction in Cases of Default of Appearance
issuer: The South China Sea Arbitration: A Chinese Perspective
summary: Chapter on jurisdictional duties and constraints when one party defaults, directly relevant to China’s non-participation in the South China Sea arbitration.

title: Jurisprudential Tenability of the Philippines v China Arbitration on South China Sea Disputes?
issuer: The South China Sea Arbitration: A Chinese Perspective
summary: Chapter assessing whether the Philippines v. China arbitration was jurisprudentially tenable from a Chinese legal-policy perspective.

title: The Impact of the Law of the Sea Convention on Conflict and Conflict Management in the South China Sea
summary: Article assessing how UNCLOS has shaped conflict and conflict-management possibilities in the South China Sea after its entry into force.

title: Fisheries Co-operation in the South China Sea and the (Ir)relevance of the Sovereignty Question
summary: Article arguing that fisheries cooperation may proceed despite sovereignty disputes and may offer a practical confidence-building path in the South China Sea.

title: Regional Cooperation in the South China Sea: Analysis of Existing Practices and Prospects
summary: Empirical survey of existing regional cooperation practices and prospects for broader cooperative mechanisms in the South China Sea.

title: Semi-enclosed Troubled Waters: A New Thinking on the Application of the 1982 UNCLOS Article 123 to the South China Sea
summary: Article applying UNCLOS Article 123 on semi-enclosed seas to the South China Sea and arguing for renewed cooperative approaches.

title: Three Disputes and Three Objectives—China and the South China Sea
summary: Influential article distinguishing different dispute layers and Chinese objectives in the South China Sea, useful for separating sovereignty, jurisdiction, and strategic questions.

title: China's Strategy in the South China Sea
summary: Widely cited article arguing that China pursued a delaying strategy to consolidate claims and deter competing claimant behavior in the South China Sea.

title: Between Assertiveness and Self-restraint: Understanding China's South China Sea Policy
summary: Article using internal Chinese debates to explain China’s policy mix of assertiveness and restraint in South China Sea disputes after 2010.

title: Fishing Militia, the Securitization of Fishery and the South China Sea Dispute
summary: Article challenging simplified maritime-militia narratives by situating fishing incidents within broader securitization of fisheries and regional dispute dynamics.

title: The Modern Origins of China’s South China Sea Claims: Maps, Misunderstandings, and the Maritime Geobody
summary: Historical article tracing the modern construction of China’s South China Sea claims through maps, translation, and maritime-geobody narratives.

title: The Modern Creation of China's 'Historic Rights' Claim in the South China Sea
summary: Article examining how the concept of historic rights in the South China Sea was constructed and contested in modern Chinese claim-making.

title: Cautious Bully: Reputation, Resolve, and Beijing's Use of Coercion in the South China Sea
summary: International Security article explaining when and why Beijing has used coercion in the South China Sea, with attention to reputation and resolve.

title: International Law as a Driver of Confrontation? UNCLOS and China's Policy in the South China Sea
summary: Open-access EJIL article asking whether international law and UNCLOS concepts helped constitute and enable confrontational Chinese South China Sea policy.

title: The Battle of Ideas under LOSC Dispute Settlement Procedures
issuer: The International Journal of Marine and Coastal Law
summary: Article on contestation over ideas and legal interpretation under LOSC dispute-settlement procedures, including implications of the South China Sea arbitration.

title: Compulsory Jurisdiction as the DNA of LOSC Dispute Settlement: An Evolutionary Path
issuer: The International Journal of Marine and Coastal Law
summary: Open-access article on compulsory jurisdiction under LOSC Part XV, useful for situating the South China Sea arbitration within broader dispute-settlement evolution.

title: 规则与失序：南海仲裁裁决的十年效应评估
issuer: 南京大学中国南海研究协同创新中心“海洋安全与发展”研究报告
summary: 中文研究报告，从中菲关系、区域秩序和海洋法实践三个维度评估南海仲裁裁决近十年的结构性影响。

title: Coercion and Restraint in the South China Sea: China's Differentiated Assertiveness towards the Philippines and Vietnam
issuer: Journal of Current Southeast Asian Affairs
summary: Recent article comparing China’s differentiated assertiveness toward the Philippines and Vietnam using maritime incidents and alliance dynamics in the South China Sea.
